name: Japanese Rosy Pickled Ginger (Gari)
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Unpeeled baby ginger (found in Oriental groceries) or Peeled regular ginger
(Note: Regular ginger tends to be stronger and tougher, but works quite well anyway)
Peel (if necessary), rinse, and pat dry
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the peeled ginger in a bowl and coat with about 1 tablespoon of pickling salt.
2. Leave at room temperature for 24 hours.
3. Dissolve ½ cup sugar, 2 cups unseasoned rice vinegar, and 2 tablespoons water in a separate container.
4. Place the ginger into the sugar-vinegar solution.
5. Seal the jar tightly and refrigerate for at least one week.
6. The ginger will turn a rosy pink. Slice thinly with the grain just before serving.
You can keep a jar of this going indefinitely by replenishing the ginger and vinegar/sugar mixture as needed.
